mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels in >55% of monthly observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during an economic expansion phase reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during expansion phases, or signal direction flips to negative in >40% of expansion-period observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into higher earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 310.0, persistent inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing borrowing costs and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL > 310.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent increase in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal predicts positive 20-day returns in <50% of triggered months, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales during a monetary tightening cycle signals weakening consumer demand, prompting equity deleveraging and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during tightening cycles fail to predict negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) outside tightening regimes.
